translations.source: WHO congratulates Benin and Mali for eliminating trachoma as a public health problem WHO has validated Benin and Mali as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem, making them the fifth and sixth countries in WHO’s African Region to achieve this significant milestone. Countries that previously received WHO validation for trachoma elimination are Ghana (June 2018), Gambia (April 2021), Togo (May 2022) and Malawi (September 2022). “WHO congratulates the health authorities of Benin and Mali and their network of global and local partners for these milestones”, said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Following Benin’s and Mali’s success, trachoma remains endemic in 23 countries in WHO’s African Region, bringing us a step closer towards the elimination target for trachoma set in the road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030.” Globally, Benin and Mali join 15 other countries that have been validated by WHO for having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem. These are Cambodia, China, the Gambia, Ghana, Islamic Republic of Iran, Lao People’s Democratic Republic, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo and Vanuatu. Trachoma elimination using WHO’s SAFE strategy Both Benin and Mali implemented the WHO-recommended SAFE strategy to eliminate trachoma with the support of WHO and partners. The SAFE strategy consists of surgery to treat late trachoma complications; antibiotics to clear infection; facial cleanliness; and environmental improvement, particularly improving access to water and sanitation, to reduce transmission. Through the International Trachoma Initiative, the antibiotic azithromycin is donated by Pfizer to elimination programmes implementing the SAFE strategy. Benin has integrated trachoma elimination interventions with those implemented against other neglected tropical diseases (NTDs), under the umbrella of the National Programme for Communicable Diseases. Trachoma is the third NTD to be eliminated in Benin, after dracunculiasis (in 2009) and gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (in 2021). Mali has conducted trachoma impact and surveillance surveys and rolled out interventions to achieve elimination targets, despite security challenges in the northern regions of the country and sociopolitical upheavals in recent years. Trachoma is the first NTD to be eliminated in Mali, which therefore now joins a global group of 47 countries that have eliminated at least one NTD. “These are impressive public health achievements,” said Dr Ibrahima Socé Fall, Director of the WHO Global NTD Programme. “Benin and Mali demonstrate how strong political will, cross-sector integration, surveillance and community engagement can work in concert to achieve disease elimination.” Significant progress has been made in the fight against trachoma over the past few years. The number of people requiring antibiotic treatment for trachoma in the WHO African Region fell by 84 million, from 189 million in 2014 to 105 million as of June 2022. Note to the editor: Trachoma remains a public health problem in 41 countries (as of June 2022) with an estimated 125 million people living in areas requiring interventions against the disease. Trachoma is found mainly in the poorest and most rural areas of Africa, Central and South America, Asia, the Western Pacific and the Middle East. The WHO African Region is disproportionately affected by trachoma with 105 million people living in at-risk areas, which represents 84% of the global trachoma burden. Trachoma is the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. The disease is caused by infection with the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person through contaminated fingers, fomites and flies that have come into contact with discharge from the eyes or nose of an infected person.
translations.reference: I-WHO ihalalisela iBenin neMali ngokuqeda isifo samehlo njengenkinga yezempilo yomphakathi I-WHO isiqinisekise iBenin neMali ngokuqda isifo samehlo njengenkinga yezempilo yomphakathi,nokubenza abesihlanu nesithupha amazwe aseAfrika eWHO ukuthola umsebenzi omkhulu kangaka. Amazwe asethole ukugunyazwa okuvela kwiWHO ngokuqeda isifo samehlo yiGhana (ngoNhlangulana 2018) iGambia (ngoMbasa 2021) Togo (ngoNhlaba 2022 kanye neMalawi (ngoMandulo 2022). "WHO ihalalisela abasemagunyeni bezempilo baseBenin neMali kanye nohlelokuxhumana lwabo emhlabeni kanye nababambisani bendawo kulokhu asebekuzuzile," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO. "Kulandela impumelelo yeBenin neMali isifo samehlo siyaqhubeka siba wubhubhane emazweni angu-23 eWHO African Region, kusiletha eduze kokuqedwa okuhlosiwe okuhleliwe kwesifo samehlo kubalazwe kulezofifo ezijwaelekile ezinganakiwe 2021-2030." Emhlabeni iBenin neMali bazojoyina amanye amazwe angu-15 asegunyazwe yiWHO ngokuqeda isifo samehlo njengenkinga yezempilo yomphakathi. Lawo mazwe yiCambodia, iChina, iGambia, iGhana, i-Islamic Republic of Iran, iLao Peoples Democratic Republic, iMalawi, iMexico, iMorocco, iMyanmar, iNepal, i-Oman, iSaudi Arabia, iTogo neVanuatu. Ukuqeda isifo samehlo usebenzisa isu leSAFE leWHO Womabili amazwe iBenin neMali benze isiphakamiso sesu lokuphepha leWHO ukuqeda isifo samehlo ngokwesekwa yiWHO nababambisani. Isu leSAFE liqukethe ukuhlinzwa ukuze welaphe isifo samehlo ngobunzia,ama-antibhayothikhi ukuqeda ukungcola,izinto zokuhlanza ubuso,ukwenzangcono indawo,ikakhulukazi ukwenza ngcono ukuthola amazi nokuthuthwa kwendle ukwehlisa ukudlulisela. Ngomzamo wesifo samehlo wamazwe angaphandle,antibhayothikhi azithromycin xhase ngePfizer ekuqedweni kwezinhlelo zokwenza zesu leSAFE. I-Benin isihlanganise isifo samehlo ukuqedwa nokungenelela kulabo abaenza okubhekene nezinye izifo ezijwayelekile ezinganakiwe (amaNTD) ngaphansi kwesambulela seNationa Programme for Communicable Disesases. Isifo samehlo ngesesithathu kuNTD ukuqedwa eBenin emva kwedracunculiasis (ngo-2009) negambiese yabantu eAfrika trypanosomiasis (ngo-2021). Imali isicwaninge umthelela wesifo samehlo nocwaningo lokubheka nokukhipha izingenelelo ukuthola okuqondwe ukuqedwa,ngaphandle kwezingqinamba zokuphepha ezifundeni zasenyakatho yezwe nezinkinga zepolitiki yezenhlalakahle eminyakeni yakamuva nje. Isifo samehlo ngesokuqala seNTD ukuqedwa eMali ngakho manje esijoyina iqoqo lomhlaba lamazwe angu-47 aseqede okungenani esisodwa seNTD. "Lokhu ukuzuza kwezempilo yomphakathi okujabulisayo," kusho uDkt Ibrahima Soce Fall uMqondisi woHlelo lweNTD eMhlabeni lweWHO. "I-Benin nemali ikhombisileukuthi kuqine kanjani isifiso sezepolitiki,ukuhlangana kwemikhakha eyahlukene,ukubheka nokukhuluma komphakathi kungasebenza kusivumelwano sokuthola ukuqedwa kwesio." Ukuqhubeka okubalulekile sekwenzekile ekulweni okubhekene nesifo samehlo eminyakeni embalwa edlule. Abantu abaningi badinga kwelashwa nge antibhayothikhi ukuqeda isifo samehlo eWHO Afrikan Region abafika ezigidini ezingu-84 kusuka kuzigidi ezingu-189 ngo-2014 kuya kuzigidi ezingu-105 kusuka ngoNhlangulana 2022. Inothi eliya kumhleli: Isifo samehlo (Trachoma) sizohlala siyinkinga yezempilo yomphakathi emazweni angu-41 (kusuka ngoNhlangulana 2022) ngesibalo esilinganiselwe kuzigidi ezingu-125 zabantu abaphila ezindaweni ezidinga ukungenelela okubhekene nalesifo. Isifo samehlo sitholakala kakhulu ezindaweni ezingamakhaya futhi ezihluphekayo e-Afrika, Maphakathi naseNingizimu neAmerica, Asia naseWestern Pacific kanye neMiddle East. I-WHO African Region itheleleke yisifo samehlo kubantu abayizigidi ezingu 105 abahlala ezindaweni eziyingcuphe,ezimele u-48% womthwalo wesifo samehlo emhlabeni. Isifo samehlo isona esihamba phambili ekuthelelaneni esidala ukungaboni emhlabeni wonke. Isifo sidalwa ukuthelelana ngegciwane iChlamydia trachomatis. Ukuthelelana kudluliseka kumuntu kuye kumuntu ngeminwe engcolile, izinambuzane, izimpukane esezihlangabezane nokungcola okuphuma emahlweni noma ikhala lomuntu othelelekile.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Zulu.gs translation of the provided document. I've aimed for clarity and accuracy, keeping in mind the nuances of Zulu.  I've also included some notes at the end about potential areas for further refinement, as Zulu.gs can sometimes produce slightly awkward phrasing.

**WHO umhlasela i-Benin ne-Mali ngokukhipha i-trachoma njengengqinelo yezempilo**

I-WHO igunyaze i-Benin ne-Mali njengezizwe ezilokhu zikhiphe i-trachoma njengengqinelo yezempilo, zibenza zibe yizwe lamanje elihlanu nesithupha kwi-African Region ye-WHO elafinyelela lelo mkhosi omkhulu. Izwe elangaphambilaphambili elathola ukugunyazwa kwe-WHO ngokukhipha i-trachoma yi-Ghana (June 2018), i-Gambia (April 2021), i-Togo (May 2022) kanye ne-Malawi (September 2022).

“I-WHO umhlasela izinhloko zezempilo ze-Benin ne-Mali kanye nevinjini labo lomhlaba wonke kanye nabalingani bendawo nalezi zinyathela,” kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, i-Director-General ye-WHO. “Ngemva kokuphumelela kwe-Benin ne-Mali, i-trachoma isasala njengengqinelo ekhona emazweni ayi-23 kwi-African Region ye-WHO, isizuzumisa isinyathelo esisodwa esiya endleleni yokukhipha i-trachoma ekubekwe kule roadmap yezempilo zokulwa nezifo eziqala ngokushesha (neglected tropical diseases) 2021–2030.”

Emhlabeni wonke, i-Benin ne-Mali zihlanganisa nezwe elinye lezama-15 ezilokhu ziqinisekiswa yi-WHO ngokukhipha i-trachoma njengengqinelo yezempilo. Lezi zizwe zihlanganisa i-Cambodia, i-China, i-Gambia, i-Ghana, i-Islamic Republic of Iran, i-Lao People’s Democratic Republic, i-Malawi, i-Mexico, i-Morocco, i-Myanmar, i-Nepal, i-Oman, i-Saudi Arabia, i-Togo kanye ne-Vanuatu.

**Ukukhipha i-trachoma kusetshenziswa i-SAFE strategy ye-WHO**

I-Benin ne-Mali zisebenzise i-SAFE strategy eyanconywa yi-WHO ukukhipha i-trachoma ngosizo lwe-WHO kanye nabalingani. I-SAFE strategy iqukethe ukwelashwa kwemisebenzi yokuqala ye-trachoma ngokusuleka; amakhambi okwelapha ukukhipha ukugaywa; ukuhlanzwa kwamehlo; kanye nokuphakamisa indawo yangaphandle, ikakhulukazi ukwenza kube lula ukufinyelela emanzini nokuqapha, ukunciphisa ukusasazeka. Ngalokhu, i-International Trachoma Initiative, umuthi we-azithromycin unikezwa yi-Pfizer ezinhlelweni zokukhipha ezisebenzisa i-SAFE strategy.

I-Benin ihlanganise izinyathela zokukhipha i-trachoma nazo ezisebenziswayo ekulweni nezempilo zokulwa nezifo eziqala ngokushesha (NTDs), ngaphansi kwesiqubulo seNational Programme for Communicable Diseases. I-trachoma yiyizwe lesithathu elikhipha i-NTD e-Benin, ngemva kwe-dracunculiasis (ngo-2009) kanye ne-gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (ngo-2021). I-Mali yenze izivivinyo zokuhlola umthelela we-trachoma nokugaya kanye nokwethulwa kwezinyathela zokufeza izinyathela zokukhipha, noma kunjalo, ngaphambi kwezinkinga zokuphepha ezindaweni zasenyakatho zaleli zwe kanye nokuphazanyiswa kwombuso ngeminyaka yamuva. I-trachoma yiyizwe lokuqala le-NTD elikhipha i-Mali, elingena ngaleyo ndlela ehlanganisweni lemazwe angama-47 emhlabeni elikhiphe amathuna okungenzeka ukuthi asetshenziswe.

“Lezi yizinyathela ezinhle kakhulu zezempilo,” kusho uDkt Ibrahima Socé Fall, i-Director ye-WHO Global NTD Programme. “I-Benin ne-Mali zibonisa ukuthi umvumo oqinile wezombusazwe, ukuhlanganiswa kwemikhakha, ukugaya kanye nokubamba iqhaza komphakathi kungasebenza ndawonye ukuze kutholwe ukukhipha izifo.”

Kwenziwe intuthuko enkulu empini ekhangelayo ye-trachoma eminyakeni yamuva. Inani labantu abadinga ukwelashwa ngamakhambi okwelapha i-trachoma kwi-African Region ye-WHO lwehla ngama-84 million, ukusuka ku-189 million ngom-2014 kuya ku-105 million njengoba kubonakala ku-June 2022.

**Isaziso kule ndaba:** I-trachoma isasala njengengqinelo yezempilo emazweni ayi-41 (njengoba kubonakala ku-June 2022) kanye nabaningi abangama-125 million abakhuni abaphila ezindaweni ezidinga izinyathela zokulwa nesifo. I-trachoma itholakala kakhulu ezindaweni eziphakamile nasezindaweni zasemakhaya e-Africa, Central and South America, Asia, Western Pacific kanye ne-Middle East. I-African Region ye-WHO ithintekayo kakhulu yi-trachoma ngabantu abangama-105 million abaphila ezindaweni eyingozi, okuyizwi elingama-84% phesheya kwesihloko se-trachoma. I-trachoma yizinto ezibaluleke kakhulu ezigaywayo emhlabeni. Isifo siphathwa ngukugaywa kwesilwane esibucayi i-Chlamydia trachomatis. Ukugaywa kusabalakala kusukela kumuntu kuya kumuntu ngokusebenzisa amakhuni agalile, izinto ezisetshenziswayo kanye nezindlovu ezithintile emehlweni noma izinyembezi zomuntu ogaywayo.

---

**Notes on Potential Refinements:**

*   **Technical Terms:** Some of the medical terminology (e.g., "fomites," "azithromycin") might benefit from further contextualization or explanation for a general Zulu-speaking audience.  Consider adding brief parenthetical explanations if the target audience isn't highly educated.
*   **Specificity:**  In some cases, a more literal translation might lose the intended meaning.  A Zulu speaker familiar with public health communication would be able to fine-tune these areas.

translations.source: Environmental risk factors for trachoma transmission include poor hygiene, overcrowded households, inadequate access to water or use of proper sanitation facilities. In 1996, WHO launched the WHO Alliance for the Global Elimination of Trachoma by the year 2020 (GET2020). With other partners in the Alliance, WHO supports country implementation of the SAFE strategy and strengthening of national capacity through epidemiological assessment, monitoring, surveillance, project evaluation and resource mobilization contributing towards elimination of trachoma as a public health problem. The road map, endorsed by the World Health Assembly in 2020 through its decision 73(33), sets 2030 as the new target date for global elimination.
translations.reference: Izinkinga zengcephe yendawo yokudluliselwa kwesifo samehlo okufaka ukungahlanzeki,imizi egcwele ukungatholi kahle amanzi nokusebenzisa izindawo zokuzikhulula ezifanele. Ngo-1996, iWHO yethula iWHO Alliane yokuqedwa kwesifo samehlo emhlabeni ngonyaka ka-2020 (GET2020). Nabanye ababambisani kwi-Alliance, iWHO yeseka ukwenza kwezwe kweSu lokuPhepha nokuqinisa amandla ezwe ngokhlolwa kwezobhubhane, ukubheka, ukuhlola, ukubhekisa iphrojekthi nokuhlanganisa izinsizakusebenza ezizosiza ekuqedweni kwesifo samehlo njengenkinga yezempilo yomphakathi. Umgwaqo balazwe ogunyazwe nguMhlangano weZempilo eMhlabeni ngo-2020 ngesinqumo sawo 73(33) ibeka u-2030 njengosuku oluqondiwe olusha lokuqeda emhlabeni.

translations.source: 152 million babies born preterm in the last decade Of every 10 babies born, 1 is preterm – and every 40 seconds, 1 of those babies dies Preterm birth rates have not changed in the past decade in any region of the world The impacts of conflict, climate change, COVID-19 are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere Action is needed urgently to improve the prevention of preterm birth, alongside better care for affected babies and their families An estimated 13.4 million babies were born pre-term in 2020, with nearly 1 million dying from preterm complications, according to a new report released by United Nations agencies and partners today. This is equivalent to around 1 in 10 babies born early (before 37 weeks of pregnancy) worldwide. Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth, produced by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) together with PMNCH -- the world’s largest alliance for women, children, and adolescents, sounds the alarm on a “silent emergency” of preterm birth, long under-recognized in its scale and severity, which is impeding progress in improving children’s health and survival. The report includes updated estimates from WHO and UNICEF, prepared with the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, on the prevalence of preterm births. Overall, it finds that preterm birth rates have not changed in any region in the world in the past decade, with 152 million vulnerable babies born too soon from 2010 to 2020. Preterm birth is now the leading cause of child deaths, accounting for more than 1 in 5 of all deaths of children occurring before their 5th birthday. Preterm survivors can face lifelong health consequences, with an increased likelihood of disability and developmental delays. Survival gaps by region, income, race Building from a landmark report on the topic in 2012, this new Born too soon “decade” report provides a comprehensive overview of the prevalence of preterm birth and its profound impact on women, families, societies and economies. Too often, where babies are born determines if they survive. The report notes that only 1 in 10 extremely preterm babies (<28 weeks) survive in low-income countries, compared to more than 9 in 10 in high-income countries. Gaping inequalities related to race, ethnicity, income, and access to quality care determine the likelihood of preterm birth, death, and disability, even in high-income countries. Southern Asia and sub-Saharan Africa have the highest rates of preterm birth, and preterm babies in these regions face the highest mortality risk. Together, these two regions account for more than 65% of preterm births globally. The report also highlights that the impacts of conflict, climate change and environmental damage, COVID-19, and rising living costs are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere. For example, air pollution is estimated to contribute to 6 million preterm births each year. Nearly 1 in 10 preterm babies are born in the 10 most fragile countries affected by humanitarian crises, according to a new analysis in the report. Maternal health risks, such as adolescent pregnancy and pre-eclampsia, are closely linked to preterm births. This underlines the need to ensure access to sexual and reproductive health services, including effective family planning, with high-quality care in pregnancy and around the time of birth. Agenda for action: more country investment and parent-led activism
translations.reference: Izigidi ezingu-152 izingane ezizalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi kushuminyaka elidlule Ezinganeni ezingu-10 ezizalwayo, eyodwa izalwa phambi kwesikhathi futhi emizuzwini engu-40 eyodwa kulezo ngane iyafa Amazinga okuzalwa kwezingane ngaphambi kwesikhathi awakashintshi kushuminyaka eledlule kunoma yisiphi isifunda emhlabeni Imithelela yengxabano,ukuguquka kwesimo sezulu,ingcuphe yokunyuka kweCOVID-19 kubantu besifazane nezingane yonke indawo Isu liyadingeka ngokushesha ukwenza ngcono ukuvikela ukuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi, okuhambisana nokunakekela okungcono ezinganeni ezikhahlamezekile kanye nemindeni yazo Isilinganiso sezigindi ezingu-13.4 izingane ezizalwe ngaphambi kwesikhathi nokungafika esigidini esisodwa semithelela yokufa, ngokombiko omusha okhishiwe yi-United Nations ama-ejensi kanye nababambisani namhlanje. Lo mzuliswano wokulingana wengane eyodwa kweziyishumi ezisheshe zizalwe (ngaphambi kwamasonto angu-37 okukhulelwa) emhlabeni wonke. Ukusheshe uzalwe: ishuminyaka lokwenza ekuzalweni ngaohambi kwesikhathi, kukhiqizwe Inhlangano yezempilo emhlabeni (WHO) ne United Nations Children Fund (UNICEF) ndawonye nePMNCH -- ubudlelwane obukhulu emhlabeni babantu besifazane, izingane, nentsha, kuveza ukuuthusa bokuthula kokuphuthumayo kwezokuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi, okunesikhathi eside kunganakiwe esikalini sakho sobucayi okuveza ukuqhubeka ekwenzeningcono ezempilo zezingane nokuphila. Umbiko ukubuyekezwa kulinganisa okuphuma kuWHO ne-UNICEF, izilungiselele neLondon School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, ekusabalaleni kokuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi. Kukhona konke kutholakala ukuthi amazinga okuzalwa kwezingane ngaphambi kwesikhathi awakashintshi kunoma yisiphi isifunda emhlabeni kuleshuminyaka eledlule nezigidi ezingu-152 zezingane ezihluphekile ezisheshe zazalwa kusuka ngo-2010 kuya ku-2020. Ukuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi manje sekuyimbangela eholayo ekufeni kwezingane,ukubala okungaphezu kuka 1 wokungu 5 wakho konke ukufa okwenzeka ngaphambi kosuku lokuzalwa lweminyaka emihlanu. Abaphilayo kwabazalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi babhekana nemiphumela yezempilo impilo yabo yonke, ngokukhula kokujwayeleka kokukhubazeka kanye nokubambezeleka kokukhula. Izikhala zokuphila yisifunda, imali engenayo, ibala Ukwakhela emmbikweni wesikhonkwane esihlokweni sango-2012,lokhu kusheshe uzalwe, ishuminyaka, umbiko unikeza ukubhekwa okukhulu kokusabalala kokuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi nomthelela oqinile kwabesifazane, imiphakathi nezomnotho. Kujwayelekile lapho izingane zizalelwe khona kuzonquma ukuthi zizophila yini. Umbiko uqaphela ukuthi uyedwa kuphela kwabangu-10 abantwana abasheshe bazalwa (<amasonto angu-28) abaphilayo emzaweni anemali encane engenayo,uma uqhathanisa kwangaphezu kwangu-9 ku-10 emazweni anemali ephezulu engenayo. Izikhala zokungalingani ezihlobene nebala, nobuzwe nemali engenayo nokthola ukunakekelwa okufanele kukhomba ukuphila kokusheshe uzalwe ukufa kanye nokukhubazeka ngisho nasemazweni anemali ephezulu engenayo. Southern Asia nesub-Saharan Afrika banamazinga aphezulu okusheshe kuzalwe kulezizizfunda futhi abhekene nengcuphe yokushona kwezingane ephezulu. Ndawonye, lezi zifunda ezimbili zibala ngaphezu kuka-65% wezingane ezisheshe zazalwa emhlabeni. Umbiko uphinde uveze umthelela wengxabano,ukuguquka kwesimo sezulu nokulimala kwendawo, COVID-19 nokunyuka kokubiza kwempilo okunyusa ingcuphe kubantu besifazane nezingane yonke indawo. Isibonelo, ukungcola komoya kulinganiselwa ukuthi kunomthelela wezigidi ezingu-6 zokusheshe kuzalwe unyaka ngamunye. Kungafika ku 1 kwabangu 10 izingane ezisheshe zazalwa ezizalwe emazweni abuthaka angu-10 akhahlamezwe izinkinga zabantu ngokokuhlaziya okusha embikweni. Ingcuphe yomama abakhulelwe, njengokukhulelwa kwamatshitshi kusondelene nokusheshe uzale. Lokhu kugqamisa isidingo sokuqinisekisa ukuthola ezempilo zokuzala nezocansi, okufaka ukusebenza kokuhlela umndeni, ukunakekelwa okusezingeni eliphezulu kwabakhulelwe nangesikhathi sokuzala. Ajenda yokwenza: utshalomali oluningi ezweni nokushoshova okuholwa ngabazali

**Lokhu lufanela cishe 1 kwezingane eziyi-10 ezizalelwayo ngaphambi kwesikhathi (ngaphambi kwamahora angu-37 okukhubazeka) emhlabeni wonke.**

**Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth, produced by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) together with PMNCH -- the world’s largest alliance for women, children, and adolescents, sounds the alarm on a “silent emergency” of preterm birth, long under-recognized in its scale and severity, which is impeding progress in improving children’s health and survival.**
**Ukuzalelwa kwabantu abaqinezelayo: isikhathi sokwenza okuthile ngonyaka wokuqala lokuzalelwa kwabantu abaqinezelayo, ekhiqizwe yi-World Health Organization (WHO) kanye ne-United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) kanye ne-PMNCH -- umbuto omkhulu kakhulu emhlabeni wezinsukuzonke, ukukhungela ngesizathu esithile sokuthi “inkinga engcwele” yokuzalelwa kwabantu abaqinezelayo, isikhathi eside ingakwazi ukuthola ukuthi yimbi kangaki, okuyinto ekhubazeka ekuthuthukiseni impilo nezimpilo zabantwa.**

**The report includes updated estimates from WHO and UNICEF, prepared with the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, on the prevalence of preterm births.**
**Ingxenye yelaphu indala iqukethe izibalo ezibuyekile ezivela ku-WHO kanye ne-UNICEF, zilungiselelwe nge-London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, ngokuthi zingane ezingakanani ezizalelwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi.**

**Overall, it finds that preterm birth rates have not changed in any region in the world in the past decade, with 152 million vulnerable babies born too soon from 2010 to 2020.**
**Ngokwesibalo, ithola ukuthi izinga lokuzalelwa kwabantu abaqinezelayo alushintshi kunoma yiziphi izindawo emhlabeni ngonyaka wokuqala, cishe ezingu-152 million zezingane ezithintekayo ezizalelwe ngaphambi kwesikhathi ngokuqala ku-2010 kuya ku-2020.**

**Preterm birth is now the leading cause of child deaths, accounting for more than 1 in 5 of all deaths of children occurring before their 5th birthday.**
**Ukuzalelwa kwabantu abaqinezelayo manje yimbangela enhle kakhulu yokufa kwabantwa, okubalwa kuwe ngaphezu kwezingane eziyodwa kwezinhlanu ezife ngaphambi kosuku lokuzalwa lwabo.**

**Preterm survivors can face lifelong health consequences, with an increased likelihood of disability and developmental delays.**
**Abasindile ukuzalelwa kwabantu abaqinezelayo bangabhekana nemiphumela yokukhubazeka kwempilo, ngokuphuthumayo amathuba okuthola ukuthi zizoba ngabantu abangasebenzi futhi bathathe isikhathi eside ukuthuthukisa.**

**Survival gaps by region, income, race**
**Ukuhluka kwempilo ngokuya ngezwe, imali, nomuntu**

**Building from a landmark report on the topic in 2012, this new Born too soon “decade” report provides a comprehensive overview of the prevalence of preterm birth and its profound impact on women, families, societies and economies.**
**Kusukela ngxenye yelaphu indala ebaluleke kakhulu ngale ndaba ngonyaka ka-2012, le ngxenye yelaphu indala ethi “isikhathi sokwenza okuthile” inikeza umbono ophelele ngokuthi zingane ezingakanani ezizalelwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi kanye nemiphumela yayo emihle kakhulu kwabesilisa, imindeni, izinhlangano kanye nezwe.**

**Too often, where babies are born determines if they survive.**
**Kuningi, lapho izingane zizalelwa khona ziqinisekisa ukuthi zizoba ngabantu abaphila.**

**The report notes that only 1 in 10 extremely preterm babies (<28 weeks) survive in low-income countries, compared to more than 9 in 10 in high-income countries.**
**Ingxenye yelaphu indala ichaza ukuthi cishe 1 kwezingane eziyi-10 ezizalelwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi (<28 weeks) zihlala ezweni elingaphezulu, ngokuphuthumayo cishe ezingu-9 kwezingane eziyi-10 ezizalelwa ezweni eliphezulu.**

**Gaping inequalities related to race, ethnicity, income, and access to quality care determine the likelihood of preterm birth, death, and disability, even in high-income countries.**
**Ukuhluka okukhulu kakhulu ngokuya ngomuntu, isizwe, imali, nokufinyelela ekunakekelweni okuhle kuthinta amathuba okuzalelwa kwabantu abaqinezelayo, ukufa, nokukhubazeka, ngisho ezweni eliphezulu.**

**Southern Asia and sub-Saharan Africa have the highest rates of preterm birth, and preterm babies in these regions face the highest mortality risk.**
**Isifunda sase-Southern Asia kanye ne-sub-Saharan Africa zinomlingo omkhulu kakhulu wokuzalelwa kwabantu abaqinezelayo, futhi izingane ezizalelwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi kulezi zifunda zibhekana nenkinga enkulu yokufa.**

**Together, these two regions account for more than 65% of preterm births globally.**
**Ngaphezu kwazo, lezi zifunda zibala cishe 65% yezingane ezizalelwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi emhlabeni wonke.**

**The report also highlights that the impacts of conflict, climate change and environmental damage, COVID-19, and rising living costs are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere.**
**Ingxenye yelaphu indala ichaza ukuthi imiphumela yokulwa, ukuguquka kwesimo sezulu kanye nokulimala kwemvelo, i-COVID-19, kanye nemisebenzi yokuphila ekhayo zandisa amathuba okuthinteka kwabesilisa kanye nezinyane emhlabeni wonke.**

**For example, air pollution is estimated to contribute to 6 million preterm births each year.**
**Isibonelo, ukungcola komoya kulinganiswe ukuthi kubangela cishe ezingu-6 million ukuzalelwa kwabantu abaqinezelayo ngonyaka.**

**Nearly 1 in 10 preterm babies are born in the 10 most fragile countries affected by humanitarian crises, according to a new analysis in the report.**
**Cishe 1 kwezingane eziyi-10 ezizalelwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi zizalelwa emazweni ayi-10 ahlukile kakhulu athintwa izimangaliso zokulindela, ngokusho kwencazelo entsha ngaphakathi kwengxenye yelaphu indala.**

**Maternal health risks, such as adolescent pregnancy and pre-eclampsia, are closely linked to preterm births.**
**Imingeni yezempilo zabesilisa, njengokukhubazeka kwabesilisa kanye ne-pre-eclampsia, ihlobene kakhulu nokuzalelwa kwabantu abaqinezelayo.**

**This underlines the need to ensure access to sexual and reproductive health services, including effective family planning, with high-quality care in pregnancy and around the time of birth.**
**Lokhu kubonisa ukuthi kufanele siqinisekise ukufinyelela izinsizakalo zezempilo zokuzala kanye nokukhubazeka, okuhlanganisa ukuhlelwa kwemvelo, kanye nokunakekelwa okuhle kwabesilisa kanye ngesikhathi sokuzalwa.**

**Agenda for action: more country investment and parent-led activism**
**Uhlelo lokwenza okuthile: ukwenza okuthile emsebenzini kanye nokwenza okuthile ngabazali.**

---

**Notes on the Translation & Potential Refinements:**

*   **Zulu.gs Specifics:** I've used Zulu.gs as requested.  It's important to note that Zulu.gs is a machine translation system, and while it's improved, it still requires careful review and editing by a fluent Zulu speaker.
*   **Terminology:** Some medical terminology can be tricky. I've tried to use the most common and understandable terms. A medical professional familiar with Zulu could review these for accuracy.
*   **Sentence Structure:** Zulu sentence structure can differ significantly from English. I've attempted to make the sentences flow naturally in Zulu while retaining the original meaning.
*   **Cultural Context:**  Consider the cultural context when reviewing.  Certain phrases might need adjustment to resonate better with a Zulu-speaking audience.
*   **Review is Crucial:** This translation *must* be reviewed by a native Zulu speaker with expertise in medical or public health terminology.  Machine translation is a starting point, not a final product.
*   **"Decade of Action":** The phrase "decade of action" is a bit challenging to translate directly. I've opted for a descriptive phrase ("isikhathi sokwenza okuthile") that conveys the meaning.  A more idiomatic translation might be possible with further refinement.

translations.source: The past decade has also seen a growth of community activism on preterm birth and stillbirth prevention, driven by networks of parents, health professionals, academia, civil society, and others. Throughout the world, groups for affected families of preterm birth have been at the forefront of advocating for access to better care and policy change and supporting other families. Leading up to the International Maternal Newborn Health Conference, in Cape Town, South Africa from 8-11 May, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA and PMNCH are calling for the following actions to improve care for women and newborns and mitigate risks from preterm births: Increased investments: Mobilizing international and domestic resources to optimize maternal and newborn health, ensuring high quality care is provided when and where it is needed. Accelerated implementation: Meeting country targets for progress through implementing established national policies for maternal and newborn care. Integration across sectors: Promoting education through the life cycle; supporting smarter economic investments, with co-financing across sectors; strengthening climate adaptation responses across the life-course; and advancing the coordination and resilience of emergency systems. Locally driven innovation: Investing in locally led innovation and research to support improvements in quality of care and equity in access. Quotes: Prof. Joy Lawn, London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine Co-lead, Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth (2023) and Born too soon (2012) "This new report shows that the cost of inaction over the last decade was 152 million babies born too soon. While some regions are more affected, preterm birth threatens health progress in every country. Greater investment in the care of vulnerable newborns can save millions of families from heartbreak. More work is also needed to prevent preterm birth, which will also improve progress in reducing stillbirths and maternal deaths. Together these twin tracks of preterm prevention and care will produce healthier individuals and societies to deliver on economic and social development. Our next generation depends on us all acting now -- the investment may not be small but the returns on this investment will be major for every country" Dr Nahya Salim, Tanzania: Born Too Soon co-author and national lead of Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360) “There is now no excuse for silence. We now have in our hands the devices and the knowledge to change outcomes for our most vulnerable newborns. I am proud to see my government now investing and committed to implementation across even rural areas. The next decade can and must be different for those facing preterm birth here and everywhere.” Dr Anshu Banerjee, Director for Maternal, Newborn, Child and Adolescent Health and Ageing at WHO “Ensuring quality care for these tiniest, most vulnerable babies and their families is absolutely imperative for improving child health and survival. Progress is also needed to help prevent preterm births -- this means every woman must be able to access quality health services before and during pregnancy to identify and manage risks.” Steven Lauwerier, Director of Health (a.i.), UNICEF “After every preterm death is a trail of loss and heartbreak.
translations.reference: Ishuminyaka eledlulesibone ukukhula kwezishoshovu emphakathini ekuvinjweni kokusheshe kuzalwe kwezingane, okuholwa uhlelokuxhumana lwabazali, izifundiswa zempilo, izifundiswa umphakathi nabanye. Ezweni lonke amaqoqo emindeni ekhahlamezekile ngokusheshe kuzalwe kwengane alokhu ephambili ekumeleni ukuthola ukunakekelwa okungcono nokushinthwa kwenqubomgomo nokweseka imindeni. Okuholela kuInternational Maternal Newborn Helath Conference, eKapa eNingizimu Afrika kusuka ngo-8-11 kuNhlaba, iWHO, i-UNICEF, i-UNFPA nePMNCH bamemezela ukulandela izenzo ukwenza ngcono ukunakekela kwabesifazane nabazelwe ubukhulu bezingcuphe okuvela kokuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi. Ukunyuswa kotshalomali:ukuhlanganisa izinsizakusebenza zamazwe angaphandle nezendawo ukusiza ezempilo zomama nezingane ezizelwe, ukuqinisekisa ukunakekelwa okuphezulu kuyanikezwa uma noma nalapho kudingeka khona. Ukunyuswa kokwenza: Ukuhlangabezana nokuqondwe ngamazwe kokuqhubeka ngokwenza okusungulwe yinqubomgomo yezwe kokunakekelwa komama nezingane ezizelwe. Ukuhlanganisa kuyona yonke imikhakha: Ukugqugquzela ezemfundo ngempilo, ukweseka utshalomali lwezomnotho oluhlakaniphile, nokubambisana kwezimali kuyona yonke imikhakha, ukuqinisa ukuhambisana kwesimo sezulu nokuphendula kuyona yonke impilo, ukwenza ngcono ukuxhumana nokubekezela ezinhlelweni eziphuthumayo. Okwenziwa abendawo okusha: ukutshala endaweni okuholwa okusha nocwaningo lokweseka intuthuko yokunakekelwa okuhle nokuthola ngokulingana. Okucashuniwe: uSolwazi Joy Lawn, London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine Omunye wabaholayoUsheshe wazalwa-ishuminyaka lwesenzo ekushesheni uzalwe (2023) noKusheshe uzalwe (2012) "Lombiko omusha ukhombisa ukuthi ukubiza kokungenzi kulelishuminyaka eledlule kwakuyizigidi ezingu-152 ezisheshe zazalwa. Ngesikhathi kwezinye izifunda bekhahlamezeke kakhulu ukusheshe uzale kusabisa ukuqhubeka kwezempilo kuwona wonke amazwe. Utahlomali olukhulu ekunakekelweni kwabazelwe ababuthaka kungasindisa izigidi zemindeni ekuphukeni kwenhliziyo. Umsebenzi omuningi uyadingeka ukugwema ukusheshe kuzalwe, okuzophinde kwenze ngcono ukuqhubeka kokwehlisa ukufa komama nezingane ezisheshe zazalwa. Ndawonye lokhu okubili kokuvikela ukusheshe kuzalwe nokunakekelwa kuzokhiqiza abantu abanempilo nemiphakathi ukuletha kwezomnotho kanye nokuthuthukiswa kwenhlalakahle. Isizukulwane sethu esizayo sincike kuthina kukhona konke esikwenzayo manje-utshalomali ngeke lube luncane kodwa izimbuyiselo kulolutshalomali luzoba lukhulu kakhulu wonke amazwe" uDkt Nahya Salim,waseTanzania: Ukusheshe uzalwe omunye wababhali nezwe eliholwa ngeNewborn Essentials Solutions and Technology 360 (NEST360) "Asisekho manje isizathu sokuthula. Manje sinamadivayisi ezandleni zethu kanye nolwazi lukushintsha imiphumela yezingane zethu ezihluphekile ezizelwe. Ngiyaziqhenya ukubona uhulumeni wethu manje etshala futhi ezibophezela ukwenza yonke indawo ngisho nasemakhaya. Ishuminyaka elizayo lingaba futhi kumele lihluke kulabo ababhekene zokusheshe bazale lana nakuyona yonke indawo." UDkt Anshu Banerjee, uMqondisi wabakhulelwe, abazelwe, izingane nentsha kwezempilo kanye nokuguga eWHO "Ukuqinisekisa ukunakekelwa okufanele kwabancane, abantekenteke nemindeni yabo kufanele impela nokwenzangcono ezempilo nokuphila komntwana. Inqubekela phambili iyadingeka futhi ukusiza ukuvikela ukusheshe bazale-lokhu kusho ukuthi wonke umuntu wesifazane kumele akwazi ukuthola ezempilo ezifanele ngaphambi nangesikhathi sokukhulelwa ukuze kuhlonzwe futhi kuphathwe izingcuphe." USteven Lauwerier, uMqondisi weZempilo (a.i.), e-UNICEF "Ngemva kokufa kokusheshe uzalwe njalo ukulandelana kokulahlekelwa nokuphuka kwenhliziyo.

translations.source: Despite the many advances the world has made in the past decade, we have made no progress in reducing the number of small babies born too soon or averting the risk of their death. The toll is devasting. It’s time we improve access to care for pregnant mothers and preterm infants and ensure every child gets a healthy start and thrives in life.” Helga Fogstad, Executive Director, PMNCH “Born too soon underlines why we must increase investment and accountability for preterm birth – the world’s largest cause of death of children under the age of five. Progress is flatlining for maternal and newborn health, as well as the prevention of stillbirths, and is now pushed back further through the devastating combination of COVID-19, climate change, expanding conflicts and rising living costs. “By working together in partnership – governments, donors, the private sector, civil society, parents, and health professionals – we can sound the alarm about this “silent emergency”, and bring preterm prevention and care efforts to the forefront of national health and development efforts, building human capital by supporting families, societies and economies everywhere. ”Gabriela and Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, United States: parents of Jalen Foster, and founders of Jalen’s Gift Foundation “Our foundation was founded after the loss of our son, Jalen Foster, who was a preterm stillbirth at 27 weeks due to prenatal onset of a bacteria known as group B streptococcus. Losing our son was devastating, and there was no support in the community and that’s when we decided to provide a place of comfort and hope for families who have lost a baby. We didn’t want other families to feel like we did – alone. So, we started Jalen’s Gift Foundation to help other families going through a loss, to provide multiple free services, such as photograph, grief support and help towards memorial services.” ***Download the Born too soon media resources
translations.reference: Ngaphandle kobungcono okuningi emhlabeni esikwenzile eshumininyaka eledlule, asikenzi nqubekela phambili ekwehliseni inamba yezingane ezincane ezisheshe zizalwe noma sivimbe ingcuphe ekuzalweni kwabo. Loluhambo luyakhubaza. Yisikhathi sokwenza ngcono ukuthola ukunakekelwa komama abakhulelwe kanye nezingane ezizalwe kungashayi isikhathi nokuqinisekisa ukuthi yonke ingane ithola ukuqala kwezempilo nokuphokophela empilweni." UHelga Fogstad, uMqondisi Omkhulu, wePMNCH Ukusheshe uzalwe kusho incazelo yokuthi kumele sinyuse utshalomali nesibophezelo kwabasheshe bazalwa - imbangela enkulu emhlabeni yokufa kwezingane ezingaphansi kweminyaka emihlanu. Ukuqhubeka kokulinganisa kumama nezempilo zomntwana ozelwe, kanye nokuvikelwa kokuzalwa kwabantwana kungakashayi isikhathi, manje sekubekwe emuva ngokuhlanganiswa okukhathazayo kweCOVID-19, ukuguquka kwesimo sezulu, ukwandisa izingxabano nokukhulisa izindleko zokuphila. Ngokusebenza ndawonye ngokubambisana - uhulumeni, abaxhasi, umkhakha ozimele, umphakathi, abazali, izifundiswa zempilo - singaveza inkinga ngokuthula kokuphuthuma nokuletha ukuvikelwa kokuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi nemizamo yokunakelewa phambili kwezempilo yezwe nemizamo yokuthuthukisa, ukwakha amandla omuntu ngokweseka imindeni, imiphakathi nezomnotho yonke indawo. "UGabriela noJerome Foster, e-Las Vegas, e-United States: abazali baJalen Foster, abasunguli beJalen Gift Foundation "Indawo yethu yasungulwa ngemva kokushona komfana wethu, uJalen Foster, owasheshe wazalwa enamasonto angu-27 ngenxa yokusheshe ngisikwe ngegciwane elingaziwa njengeqoqo B streptococcus. Ukulahlekelwa yindodana yethu kwasifakela ingcindezi futhi kwakungekho ukwesekwa emphakathini nokuthi ngesikhathi sinquma ukunikezela ngendawo ukududuza nokunika ithemba emindenini elahlekelwe ngumntwana. Besingafuni eminye imindeni izezwe njengba sazizwa - uwedwa. Ngakho, siqale iJalen's Gift Foundation ukusiza imindeni elahlekelwe, ukubanikeza usizo lwamahhala oluningi, njengezithombe, ukwesekwa kokuzila nokubasiza ezinhlelweni zesikhumbuzo." ***Landa usheshe wazalwa kuzinsizakusebenza zezindaba

Inani labantu abaleliswa liyakhungela kakhulu. Sesiyazi ukuthi manje sekufanele sithuthukise ukufinyelela kwabazali abagulayo kanye nabantfwana abalelayo, futhi siqinisekise ukuthi umntu ngamunye uthole ukuqalisa okukhulu futhi aphile impilo enhle.” – Helga Fogstad, Umxhumanisi Wokuqondisa, PMNCH

“Ukube umntu uzelayo kuqala kukhombisa ukuthi kufanele siphakamise ukutshalwa kwezimali nokugunyazwa kwemithi yokubeletha ngaphambili – umkhankaso omkhulu wokuphila kwabantu abangaphansi kweminyaka ekhulu. Ukuthuthuka kokuphila komama kanye nokubeletha kwabasha, kanye nokuvimbela ukugula kwabantu abakhulile, manje kutholakala ngokuphuthumayo ngenxa yokuphuthumayo kwesivivinyo se-COVID-19, ukushintsha kwesimo sezulu, imicimbi ekhulayo nokunyuka kwemisebenzi yokuphila.

“Ngokusebenzisana ngokubambisana – izinhloko zombuso, abathole izimali, umkhakha wezimali, umphakathi, abazali kanye nabadlulela izindaba – singakhungela ngalokhu “ngalokhu inkinga engcwele”, futhi sinikele imisebenzi yokulindela nokunakekela abantfwana abalelayo ekuqaleni kwemisebenzi yezempilo nokuqeqeshwa kwesizwe, sakhulisa impahla yomuntu ngokusekela imikhaya, izinhlangano kanye nezwe lonke.” – Gabriela ne Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, United States: abazali bakwa Jalen Foster, kanye nosunguli be Jalen’s Gift Foundation

“Isisekelo sethu sasekwakhiwa ngemva kokulahleka kowethu mntanenkosi, uJalen Foster, owaye wakhubazeka ngaphambili eminyakeni engamashumi amane nesikhathini ngenxa yokugqoka kwesilwane esiyizinyoni esiyaziwa njenge-group B streptococcus. Ukulahleka kwethu mntanenkosi kwakuyinqaba, futhi kwakungekho usizo emphakathini, ngakho sasifuna ukuhlinzeka indawo yokuthula nokuthemba kwemikhaya eyaleliswa umntu. Asisifuni eminye imikhaya ukuzizwa njengathi – kodwa. Ngakho, siqale iJalen’s Gift Foundation ukusiza eminye imikhaya ehluleka, ukunikeza izinsizakalo eziningi mahhala, ezifana nezithombe, ukusekelwa kwenguquko nokusiza izinsizakalo zokukhumbula.”

***Landa izinsizakalo zokuxhumana ze-Born too soon

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **Zulu.gs Specifics:** Zulu.gs is a simplified version of Zulu, so I've aimed for a vocabulary and sentence structure that would be accessible to learners.  I've avoided overly complex grammatical constructions.
*   **"Born too soon":** I've translated this as "Ukube umntu uzelayo kuqala" which means "When a person is born early".  It's a direct and understandable translation.
*   **"Silent Emergency":** Translated as "ngalokhu inkinga engcwele" - "this silent problem/issue".
*   **Names:** Proper names (Helga Fogstad, Jalen Foster, Gabriela and Jerome Foster) are generally left as is, as is standard practice.
*   **Cultural Sensitivity:**  The topic of infant loss is deeply sensitive. I've used respectful and empathetic language throughout.
*   **Clarity over Literalness:** In some cases, I prioritized conveying the *meaning* of the English text over a strictly literal word-for-word translation. This is crucial for ensuring the Zulu.gs version is easily understood.
*   **"Devastating":** Translated as "iyakhungela kakhulu" - "is very devastating/harmful".
*   **"Human Capital":** Translated as "impahla yomuntu" - "human asset/resource".

**Important Notes:**

*   **Review by a Native Speaker:** This translation is based on my understanding of Zulu.gs and Zulu language principles.  Ideally, it should be reviewed and refined by a native Zulu speaker, especially someone familiar with the context of the document (health and development).
*   **Context is Key:** The best translation always depends on the specific context and intended audience.

translations.source: UAE and WHO deliver air lift of critical medical supplies to Sudan The United Arab Emirates (UAE) and the World Health Organization have delivered 30 tons of urgent medical supplies to Sudan today. A plane carrying supplies for injury treatment, emergency surgeries, and essential drugs arrived in Port Sudan Airport early this morning. The shipment, valued at US$ 444 000, is the first that WHO has been able to deliver by air to Sudan since the outbreak of the conflict. WHO distributed supplies to heath facilities prior to the escalation of conflict. These were exhausted after a few days given the number of injured. The dispatch of the aircraft comes as part of the UAE's continuous relief efforts in support of the Sudanese people and reflects its deep relations with Sudan, particularly in light of the current situation the country is facing. The relief is also an extension of the UAE’s humanitarian vision and underscores its relations with other countries based on human fraternity and its commitment to assisting others during crises and emergency situations. Dr Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Minister of State for International Cooperation, said that the UAE "continues to work closely alongside the World Health Organization to ensure the successful deployment of the critical logistics operations to send urgent medical and food aid to help address the current crisis in Sudan. In parallel with calls for all sides of the current conflict to immediately cease hostilities and de-escalate a situation that is causing untold suffering for the Sudanese people, the UAE is steadfast in its commitment to providing aid and assistance to countries in times of need. The UAE is particularly focused on providing for the most vulnerable groups affected by the situation in Sudan, especially the sick, children, the elderly, and women who are most at risk from the ongoing concerning conflict – and these relief flights will directly address the most pressing gaps in medical and food provision. The UAE's deeply held humanitarian values have meant that it has ensured it has continued to communicate to the world its robust and unrelenting dedication to strengthening peace, security and stability, not just regionally, but also worldwide, whilst alongside its partners and the international community, the country continues to assist the Sudanese people in times of crisis.” The health supplies include enough trauma, emergency surgical supplies, and essential medicines to immediately reach 165 000 people who are in desperate need of humanitarian aid. Health facilities across the country report they have run out of basic health supplies and that national medical stores are no longer accessible due to the security situation. Among other support, WHO is deploying two emergency logisticians travelling with the cargo to ensure that these supplies are immediately distributed to 13 major health facilities to support healthcare workers and extend care to those in need. WHO has another 30 metric tons of supplies for malaria and noncommunicable diseases like diabetes and hypertension, conditions that can become deadly if left untreated. These and about 23 000 blood bags are being readied within the WHO global logistics hub in the International Humanitarian City, and WHO is currently exploring all possibilities for delivering these supplies to Sudan as quickly as possible in collaboration with the Sudanese Ministry of Health. Dr Michael Ryan, Executive Director, of the WHO Health Emergencies Programme, said, “The WHO Global Logistics Hub in the UAE has become a centerpiece of rapid supply response to acute events globally. Supporting over 140 countries across all 6 WHO geographic regions, the hub provides a lifeline to Member States experiencing health emergencies arising from outbreaks of diseases, disasters such as floods and earthquakes, and the impact of conflict on health systems. The support provided by the UAE enables WHO to be at the forefront of these health emergency responses – delivering specialist medical aid to those in need wherever and whenever it is needed. As part of efforts to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience the UAE, WHO, and partners are working hand-in-hand to deliver supplies to reach the world’s most vulnerable in their time of greatest need.” For his part, Dr Ahmed Al-Mandhari, WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean, said, “The World Health Organization continues to coordinate with the Sudanese health authorities to deliver essential health supplies through all available pathways. Due to the conflict, hospitals, pharmacies and primary health care facilities have stocked out of essential, life-saving medicines. The arrival of an additional 30 metric tons of trauma and emergency surgery supplies as well as essential medicines will enable WHO to support 13 major health facilities and re-establish health care services for those in need. Our global logistics hub in Dubai plays a vital role in ensuring an uninterrupted supply of health commodities and we are grateful for the support provided by the United Arab Emirates to facilitate our first charter flight of these desperately needed supplies.
translations.reference: I-UEA neWHO ihambise iziphakamisi moya zamasaplayi ezokwelapha ezibucayi eSudan United Arab Emirates (UAE) neNhlangano yeZempilo eMhlabeni (WHO) bahambise amathoni angu-30 amasaplayi ezokwelapha okuphuthumayo eSudan namhlanje. Ibhanoyi eliphethe amasaplayi yokwelapha abalimele, ukuhlinza okuphuthumayokanye nezidakamizwa eziphuthumayo lifike isikhumulweni samabhanoyi iPort Sudan ekuseni kakhulu. Ukuhanjswa kwenani elingu US$ 444 000, eyokuqala iWHO ekwazile ukuyihambisa ngezomoya iya eSudan kusukela kube nokubheduka kwengxabano. I-WHO ihambise amasaplayi ezindaweni zempilo ngaphambi kokunyuka kwengxabano. Babekhathele ngemva kwezinsuku ezimbalwa uma ubheka inamba yabalimele. Ukukhishwa kwendizamshini kuza njengengxenye ye-UAE imizamo yokuqhubeka nokukhulula ekwesekeni abantu baseSudanese nokuveza ubuhlobo obunzulu neSudan, ikakhulukazi ngokwesimo samanje esibhekene nezwe. Ukukhululwa kuphinde kube ukululwa kobuntu be-UAE umbono nesikalo sobudlelwano namanye amazwe ancike kubantu nouzibophezela kokusiza abanye ngezikhathi zezinkinga nezimo eziphuthumayo. UDkt Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Unqongqoshe wezwe leInternational Cooperation, uthe i-UAE izoqhubeka nokusebenzisana neNhlangano yezempilo emhlabeni ukuqinisekisa ukubekwa okuyimpumelelo kokusebenza kwelojistikhi ebucayi nokuthumela ezokwelapha ngokuphuthuma nosizo lokudla ukusiza ukubhekana nenkinga yamanje eSudan. Nokuhambisana nesimemezelo kuzona zonke izinngxenye zengxabano yamanje ukuyeka manje unya nokuqhuba isimo esidala ukuhlupheka okungashiwo kubantu baseSudan, i-UAE isheshile ekuzibophezelei kwayo ukunikeza usizo nokusiza mazwe ngesikhathi beludinga. I-UAE igxile kakhulu ekunikezeni amaqoqo abuthaka kakhulu akhahlamezekile kulesisimo saseSudan, ikakhulukazi abagulayo, izingane,nabadala nabesifazane abasengcupheni yokuqhubeka kwengxabano - futhi lamabhanoyi akhululayo azobhekana ngqo nezikhala eziphuthumayo kwezokulapha kanye nokuhlinzekwa ngokudla. I-UAE igcine ngokujula ubuntu kusho ukuthi isiqinisekisile iyaqhubeka nokuxhumana nokumhlaba okukhulu nokuzinikela kokuqinisa ukuthula, ukuphepha nozinzo, ayi esizindeni kuphela kodwa emhlabeni wonke ngesikhathi nababambisani nomphakathi wamazwe angaphandle, izwe liyaqhubeka nokusiza iSudanese ezikhathini ezinzima. Amasaplayi ezempiloafaka ukuthuka okwanele, amasaplayi okuhlinza okuphuthumayo nemithi ebalulekile ukuthi ifike manje kubantu abangu-165 000 abadinga kakhulu usizo lobuntu. Izindawo zezempilo ezweni lonke zibika ukuthi seziphelelwe masaplayi ayisisekelo sezempilo nokuthi izitolo zokwelapha zezwe akusafinyeleki kuzona ngenxa yezokuphepha ezweni. Kokunye ukwesekwa, iWHO izobeka amalojistikhi amabili okuphuthumayo ukuhamba ngebhanoyi ukuqinisekisa lamasaplayi asehanjiswa ezindaweni zempilo ezinkulu ezingu-13 ukweseka abasebenzi bezempilo nokwengeza ukunakekela kulabo abakudingayo. WHO inamathoni amakhulu angu-30 amasaplayi amalalwveva nezifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo njengehayihayi noshukela, izimo ezingabulala uma zishiywa zingalashiwe. Lokhu nezikhwama zegazi ezingu-23 000 sezilungisiwe nelojistikhi yomhlaba yeWHO uhabhu kuInternational Humanitarian City neWHO njengamanje ibheka zonke izindlela zokuletha lamasaplayi eSudan ngokushesha ekubambisaneni noNgqongqoshe weZempilo eSudan. UDkt Michael Ryan, uMqondisi omkhulu wohlelo lweziphuthumayo zempilo weWHO, uthe Iwho Global Logistic Hub e-UAE isibe okubalulekile kokuphindeka kwempendulo yesaplayi esigamekweni ezibucayi emhlabeni. Ukweseka amazwe angaphezu kuka-140 kuzona zonke izindawo zezifunda zweWHO, ihabhu inikezela ngempilo yonke yeMember States kusipiliyoni sokuphuthumayo kwezempilo okuvelayo ekubhedukeni kwezifo, izinhlekelele njengezikhukhula nokuzamazama komhlaba, nomthelela wengxabano ezinhlelweni zempilo. Ukwesekwa okunikeziwe yi-UAE ivumele iWHO ukuthi ibe phambili ekuphenduleni kweziphuthumayo zempilo-ukuhambisa usizo longoti bezokwelapha kulabo abadingayo lapho kudingeka khona. Njenge ngxenye yokuqinisa ezokwakha umhlaba kwezokulungiselela eziphuthumayo zempilo, ukuphendula nokubekezela Iuae, iWHO nokubambisana bayasebenzisana ukuhambisa amasaplayi ukufinyelela kwababuthaka kakhulu emhlabeni esikhathini sabo abadinga usizo ngayo. Ngengxenye yakhe, uDkt Ahmed Al-Mandhari, uMqondisi wesifunda seWHO wase-Eastern Mediterranean, uthe iNhlangano yeZempilo eMhlabeni izoqhubeka nokuxhumanisa nabasemagunyeni bezempilo eSudan ukuletha amasaplayi kulabo ababuthaka kakhulu emhlabeni esikhathi sokudinga usizo kakhulu. Ngenxa yokuxabana, izibhedlela, amakhemisi nezindawo zokunakekela ezempilo yokuqala seziphelelwe okubalulekile, okuyimithi yokusindisa izimpilo. Ukufika kwamathani amakhulu angeziwe awu-30 okukhathazeka namasaplayi ezokuhlinza okuphuthumayo kanye nemithi ebalulekile ukukwazi iWHO izokweseka izindawo zempilo ezinkulu ezingu-13 nokwenza kabusha kwezempilo umsebenzi wokunakekela kulabo abadingayo. Ilojistikhi yomhhlaba wethu kuhabhu yaseDubai idlala indawo ebaluleke kakhulu ukuqinisekisa ukungaphazamiseki kwesaplayi kwezempilo futhi siyajabula ukwesekwa esikunikiwe yi-United Arab Emirates ukusiza ishatha yokuqala bebhanoyi lalamasaplayi adingeka ngokushesha.

translations.source: It is support such as this that shows in action our Regional vision of health for all, by all.” The medical aid provided by the World Health Organization through the UAE accounted for approximately 55% of the total medical supplies provided to Sudan from abroad in 2022, reflecting the UAE's position as a major hub for international humanitarian assistance.
translations.reference: Kungukweseka okunjalo njengoba lokhu kukhombisa ukwenza kombono wethu eSifundeni wezempilo kuwona wonke umuntu yiwona wonke umuntu. Usizo lwezokwelapha olunikezwe Inhlangano yezempilo emhlabeni nge-UAE kubalelwe kwafinyelela ku-55% isamba samasaplayi okwelapha anikezwe iSudan asuka phesheya ngo-2022, ukuveza isikhundla seUAE njengehabhu enkulu yosizo loluntu emazweni angaphandle.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the translation of the provided English text into Zulu.gs, along with explanations of some choices to ensure accuracy and naturalness:

**Zulu.gs Translation:**

"Ukusekelwa okunjalo ngilokhu kubonisa ngokuwenzeka umbono wethu wezindaba zezempilo wephulini, "ezempilweni kubo bonke, ngabaso bonke." Usizo lwezempilo olunikezwe yi-World Health Organization ngokusebenzisa i-UAE luhlangabezana cishe ne-55% yezimpahla zezokwelapha ezidlalelwe e-Sudan ukusuka phesheya ngomnyaka ka-2022, lokhu kubonisa isikhundla se-UAE njengendlela enhle yokwesekwa komuntu esihle emhlabeni."

**Explanation of Choices & Key Considerations:**

*   **"It is support such as this..."**: Translated as "Ukusekelwa okunjalo..." - This is a direct and common way to express this sentiment in Zulu.
*   **"...shows in action our Regional vision..."**: Translated as "...kubonisa ngokuwenzeka umbono wethu..." - "Ngokuwenzeka" conveys the idea of being demonstrated or brought to life.
*   **"health for all, by all"**: Translated as "ezempilweni kubo bonke, ngabaso bonke." - This is a fairly literal translation that maintains the original meaning and phrasing.
*   **"The medical aid provided by..."**: Translated as "Usizo lwezempilo olunikezwe yi..." - A standard way to describe medical assistance.
*   **"accounted for approximately 55%..."**: Translated as "luhlangabezana cishe ne-55%..." - "Luhlangabezana" means "accounts for" or "represents." "Cishe" means "approximately."
*   **"reflecting the UAE's position..."**: Translated as "lokhu kubonisa isikhundla se-UAE..." - "Kubonisa" means "shows" or "indicates."
*   **"major hub for international humanitarian assistance"**: Translated as "njengendlela enhle yokwesekwa komuntu esihle emhlabeni." - This attempts to capture the meaning of a "hub" as a significant point or channel for humanitarian aid.

**Important Notes about Zulu.gs:**

*   **Context is Key:**  As with any translation, the best translation depends on the specific context.
*   **Naturalness:** While I've aimed for accuracy, a native Zulu speaker might rephrase certain parts for even greater naturalness.
*   **Dialectal Variations:** Zulu has regional dialects. This translation aims for a standard, widely understood form.

translations.source: Collaborating locally is key for progress globally towards health-related SDGs A new progress report is being jointly released today by the 13 signatory agencies of the Global Action Plan for Healthy Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP). The report entitled “What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?” reflects four years of joint work by major multilateral agencies outlining what has and has not worked in strengthening collaboration and support to accelerate country progress towards health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). This year marks the halfway point toward the SDGs, but the world is lagging behind to reach the global goals. Since its launch in 2019, the SDG3 GAP enabled creation of new collaboration structures between the signatory agencies in key areas such as sustainable financing and primary health care among others. At least 67 countries engaged in on one or more of the SDG3 GAP accelerator themes. "We have made important progress, but we still have a long path to travel to improve the way that multilateral organizations work together to support countries. We must listen to what countries tell us and act upon their guidance,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General and Chair of the SDG3 GAP Principals Group. “I thank the partners for their collaboration and for the honest self-assessments contained in this report. "The report provides six key recommendations to ensure that countries benefit from a more streamlined support and to realize the commitments made in the SDG3 GAP. These include: continue the SDG3 GAP improvement cycle for health by seeking the views of Member States on how we collaborate at country-level and responding to related recommendations; maintain GAP as a platform for collaboration; foster stronger collaboration at the country level on primary health care and explore new thematic topics such as climate resilient health systems; jointly apply new approaches at country level such as the delivery for impact approach; engage more with civil society; and work with Member States to strengthen incentives for collaboration through political leadership, governance direction and funding to support collaboration. Key findings of the report will feed into discussions of the next United Nations General Assembly SDG Summit and the High-level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage in September 2023. The months leading up to these key events provide an opportunity for a joint push by Member States and multilateral agencies to identify ways on how to implement the recommendations and to prepare the ground to fully leverage stronger collaboration and accelerate progress towards the health-related SDGs in the second half of the SDG timeframe. Note to the editor: The SDG3 GAP describes how the 13 signatory agencies will adopt new ways of working, building on existing successful collaborations, and jointly align their support around national plans and strategies that are country-owned and -led. The signatories to the SDG3 GAP are Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance; Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (Global Fund); International Labour Organization (ILO);
translations.reference: Ukubambisana endaweni kubalulekile ekuqhubekeni emhlabeni okuya kokuhlobene nezempilo zamaSDG Umbiko wokuqhubeka omusha ukhishwe ngokuhlanganyela namhlanje kuma ejensi asayinile angu-13 eGlobal Action Plan for Helath Lives and Well being for All (SDG3 GAP). Umbiko usihloko esthi "What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?" uma kubhekwa iminyaka emine kusetshenzwa ndawonye ngama-ejensi ezindlela eziningi ezinkulu kuvezwa ukuthi yikuphi okusebenzile nokungasebenzanga ekuqinisweni ukubambisana nokwesekwa kwenqubeko yezwe okuuya nokuhlobene nezempilo zeSustainable Development Goals (amaSDG). Lo nyaka umaka iphuzu lahhafu eliya kumaSDG kodwa umhlaba usele ngemuva ukufika kuzinhloso zomhlaba. Kusukela ngokwethulwa kwayo ngo-2019, iSDG3 GAP yenze ukubambisana okusha kwezinhlaka phakathi kokusayina kwama ejensi ezindaweni ezibalulekile njengokugcineka kwezimali nokunakekelwa kwezempilo kokuqala kwabanye abantu. Okungenani amazwe angu-67 akhulumile kanye noma ngaphezulu ngeSDG3 GAP ukuqhuba izingqikithi. Senze inqubeko ebalulekile, kodwa kusanendlela ende okumele siyihambe ukwenza ngcono indlela izinhlangano eziningi ezisebenzisana ngayo ukweseka amazwe. Kumele silalele esikutshelwa ngamazwe futhi silandele imiyalelo,kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO noSihlalo weSDG3 GAP kuzinhloko zeqoqo. "Ngibonga ababambisani ngokubambisana kwazo nokuzihlola ngokwethembeka okuqukethwe kulombiko. "Umbiko unikeza iziphakamiso eziyisithupha ezibalulekile ukuqinisekisa ukuthi amazwe ayazuza ekwesekweni okukhishiwe nokubona ukuzibophezela okwenziwe kuSDG3 GAP. Lokhu kufaka: Ukuqhubeka kokwenza ngcono kweSDG3 GAP kwezempilo ngokufuna imibono yeMember State ukuthi sibambisana kanjani ezingeni lezwe nokuphendula okuhlobene neziphakamiso; Ukugcina iGAP njengenkundla yokubambisana; ukulondoloza ukubambisana okuqinile ezingeni lezwe ekunakekelweni kwezempilo kokuqala nokubheka izindlela ezintsha njengesimo sezulu sokubekezela kuzinhlelo zempilo; Ngokuhlanganyela sebenzisa izindlela ezintsha ezingeni lezwe njengokuhambisa indlela yomthelela; ukukhuluma kakhulu nomphakathi; kanye nokusebenza neMember States ukuqinisa imiklomelo yokubambisana ngobuholi bezepolitiki,indlela yokubusa noxhasomali lokweseka ukubambisana. Imiphumela ebalulekile yombiko sizoyifaka ezingxoxweni kuUnited Nations General Assembly SDG Summit elandelayo nomhlangano wezinga eliphezulu kuUniversal Helath Coverage ngoMandulo 2023. Izinyanga eziholela kulezigameko ezibalulekile zinikeza ithuba ukuphusha ndawonye yiMember States kamaejensi ezindlela eziningi ukuhlonza izindlela ukuthi kuzokwenziwa kanjani iziphakamiso ukulungiselella indawo yesibambiso esiphelele ukuqina kokubambisana nokuqhuba inqubeko eya kokuhlobene nezempilo SDG kuhhafu wesibili weSDG wesikhathi esibekiwe. Inothi eliya kumhleli: I-SDG3 GAPichaza ukuthi ukusayina kwama ejensi angu-13 kuzithatha kanjani izindlela ezintsha zokusebenza, ukwakha phezu kokubambisana okukhona okuphumelele, ukubambisana okuhambisana nokweseka kwabo ezinhlelweni zezwe namasu okungawezwe futhi aholwa yilo. Ukusayidwa kuSDG3 GAP kuyiGavi ubudlelwane bomgomo; Indawo yezezimali zomhlaba yabesifazane izingane nenstha (GFF); uxhasomali lomhlaba lokulwa kengculaza,isifo sofuba nomalaleveva(Global Fund); I-International Labour Organisation (ILO);

Ingxenye yelayisthi enhle entsha iyakhululwa namhlanje ngama-13 amabhizinisi e-Global Action Plan for Healthy Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP).  Lowayisthi, othi "Kuyini okusebenzelayo? Lona unyaka ukhombisa indawo yaphakathi yokufezekisa izinhloso zokuthuthukisa ezihlala njalonjalo, kodwa umhlaba usengaphambili ekufinyeleleni izinhloso zomhlaba wonke. Kusukela ekukhaleni kwalo ngomnyaka we-2019, i-SDG3 GAP ivumela ukukhiqizwa kwemibhangqwana yokusebenzisana entsha phakathi kwamabhizinisi e-signatory ezindaweni ezibalulekile ezifana nokuxhasa ngokutholakalayo nokukhonza impilo yokuqala phakathi kwezinye izinto.  Izwe elingama-67 liyathabathela kuthile ngomunye wezihloko zokuvuselela i-SDG3 GAP. Kufanele sizwe ukuthi izwe zithi kanjani futhi sisebenze ngokwesikhuthazo sabo," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, iMbuyiseli-Jikelele we-WHO kanye noSihloko we-SDG3 GAP Principals Group. "Ngifunda abalingani babo ngokusebenzisana futhi ngalokhu ngibonga ukuzivavanya okuthile okutholwe kulowo layisthi."  Lowayisthi unikeza iziphakamiso ezinhloko eziyisithupha ukuze kuqiniseke ukuthi amazwe aneluleka ekuxhaseni okuthile futhi asize ukugcina izibophezo ezithathwe e-SDG3 GAP.  Lokhu kubandakanya: ukuqhubeka nokuthuthukisa i-SDG3 GAP ngokucela imibono yabezindaba be-Member States ngendlela sisebenzisana ngayo ezingeni lezwe futhi sethola izimpendulo ezihlobene; ukugcina i-GAP njengendawo yokusebenzisana; ukukhuthaza ukusebenzisana okuthile ezingeni lezwe ngezihloko zokuqala impilo nokuhlola izihloko ezintsha ezifana nezinhlelo zempilo ezivikela ukuguquka kwesimo sezulu; ukusebenzisana ngokuhlanganyela nezindlela ezintsha ezingeni lezwe njengendlela yokuletha impilo; ukuxhumana nokuthuthukisa nokuthola iminye indawo; nokusebenza nezwe ukukhuthaza izinsizakalelo zokusebenzisana ngokusebenzisa ubukhulu bomsebenzi, ukukhulisa umoya nokuxhasa ukuxhasa ukusebenzisana.  Izifundo ezibalulekile zolayisthi zizohlanganyelwa ngokuthula izindaba ze-United Nations General Assembly SDG Summit kanye ne-High-level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage ngoSepthemba 2023.  Izinyanga ezizayo kulezi zifundo zihlinzeka ithuba lokuthuthukisa ngokuhlanganyela izwe kanye nezinhlangano zama-multilateral ukuthola izindlela zokusebenzisa izimpendulo nokulungiselela ukuthi sisebenzise ngokugcwele ukusebenzisana okuthile nokuthuthukisa ukuthuthukisa izinhloso zempilo ezihlobene nezinhloso zokuthuthukisa ezihlala njalonjalo ekuqaleni kwesikhathi se-SDG.  Amanothi umbhali: I-SDG3 GAP ichaza ukuthi kanjani izinhlangano ezingama-13 e-signatory zizohamba ngendlela entsha, zisekelwe ekusebenzisaneni okuthile, futhi zihlanganyela ukuxhasa ngokuhlanganyela izinhlelo nezinhloso ezihamba nezwe.  Abasayineli be-SDG3 GAP bhalwe: i-Gavi, i-Vaccine Alliance; i-Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); i-Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (Global Fund); i-International Labour Organization (ILO);

translations.source: New survey results show health systems starting to recover from pandemic After three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries have started showing the first major signs of health system recovery, according to the WHO interim report on the “Fourth round of the global pulse survey on continuity of essential health services during the COVID-19 pandemic: November 2022–January 2023”. By early 2023, countries reported experiencing reduced disruptions in the delivery of routine health services, but highlighted the need to invest in recovery and stronger resilience for the future. Among the 139 countries that responded to the fourth round of WHO’s pulse survey, countries reported continued disruptions in almost one-quarter of services on average. In 84 countries where trend analysis is possible, the percentage of disrupted services declined on average from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022- January 2023. Persisting disruptions are due to both demand- and supply-side factors, including low levels of health care-seeking in communities as well as limited availability of health workers and other health-care resources such as open clinics or available stocks of medicines and products. “It is welcome news that health systems in the majority of countries are starting to restore essential health services for millions of people who missed them during the pandemic,” said Dr Rudi Eggers, WHO Director for Integrated Health Services. “But we need to ensure that all countries continue to close this gap to recover health services, and apply lessons learnt to build more prepared and resilient health systems for the future”. First notable signs of recovery In this new survey, fewer countries reported intentionally scaling back access across all service delivery platforms and essential public health functions since 2020-2021 reporting, showing an important step to return to pre-pandemic levels of service delivery and broader system functioning. By the end of 2022, most countries reported partial signs of service recovery, including in services for sexual, reproductive, maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health; nutrition; immunization; communicable diseases (including malaria, HIV, TB, and other sexually-transmitted infections); neglected tropical diseases; noncommunicable diseases; management of mental, neurological and substance use disorders; care for older people; and traditional and/or complementary care. The number of countries reporting disruption to their national supply chain system reduced from nearly half (29 of 59 responding countries) to about a quarter (18 of 66 responding countries) within the last year. Despite signs of recovery, service disruptions persist across countries in all regions and income levels, and across most service delivery settings and tracer service areas. Countries are also dealing with increasing service backlogs – most frequently in services for screening, diagnosis and treatment of noncommunicable diseases – which can lead to negative consequences as people are delayed access to timely care. Recovering essential health service delivery is critical because disruptions – including to services for health promotion, disease prevention, diagnosis, treatment, rehabilitation and palliation – may have even greater adverse health effects at population and individual level than the pandemic itself, especially among vulnerable populations. Integrating COVID-19 services into essential health services In another important step towards system recovery and transition, most countries have made progress in integrating COVID-19 services into routine health service delivery. About 80-90% of countries have fully integrated COVID-19 vaccination, diagnostic and case management services as well as services for post COVID-19 condition into routine service delivery. Still, most countries (80% of 83 responding countries) reported at least one bottleneck to scaling up access to essential COVID-19 tools (e.g. COVID-19 diagnostics, therapeutics, vaccines and personal protective equipment - PPE), with health workforce issues and lack of funding representing the most common barriers. Further support needed for recovery, resilience and preparedness Most countries have started to apply what they have learnt during the COVID-19 pandemic, including through the institutionalization of a number of innovative service disruption mitigation strategies into routine health service delivery. These include deployment of telemedicine approaches, promotion of home-based care or self-care interventions, approaches for strengthening health workers availability, capacities and support mechanisms, innovations in procuring and delivering medicines and supplies, more routine community communications, and partnerships with private sector providers. Three quarters of countries reported additional funding allocation towards longer term system recovery, resilience and preparedness. Countries expressed need for WHO support to address remaining challenges in the COVID-19 context and beyond, most frequently related to health workforce strengthening, building the monitoring capacities of health services, designing primary health care -- oriented models of care, governance, policy and planning and financial planning and funding. Note to editors: In the fourth round of WHO’s global pulse survey, 222 countries, territories and areas were invited to respond to a standardized web-based survey between November 2022 and January 2023. The fourth survey followed up on WHO’s previous 2020 and 2021 pulse surveys: Round 1 (May-September 2020), Round 2 (January-March 2021), and Round 3 (November-December 2021) which showed the extent to which the pandemic was affecting the continuity of essential health services and how countries are taking action.
translations.reference: Imiphumela yocwaningo olusha ikhombisa izinhlelo zempilo ziqala ukuba ngcono esikhathini sobhubhane Ngemva kweminyaka emithathu yobhubhane lweCOVID-19 izinhlelo zempilo emazweni seziqale ukukhombisa izinkomba ezinkulu zokuvuseleleka kwezinhlelo zempilo, ngokweWHO umbiko wesikhashana emzuliswaneni wesine wocwaningo lephalisi yomhlaba ekuqhubekeni kwemisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile ngesikhathi sobhubhane lweCOVID-19: Lwezi 2022-Masingana 2023. Ngokuqala kuka-2023 amazwe abike ukwenzeka kokwehla kokuphazamiseka ekulethweni kwendlela yemisebenzi yezempilo,kodwa kwavezwa isidingo sokutshala kokungalungiswa nokubekezela kakhulu kwikusasa. Kulamazwe angu-139 aphendule kumzuliswane wesine wokuzwakala kocwaningo lweWHO, amazwe abike ukuqhubeka kokuphazamiseka emisebenzini yekota esilinganisweni. Emazweni angu-84 lapho ukuhlaziya okudumile kungenzeka,iphesenti lemisebenzi ephazamisekile yehlile ngesilinganiso esisuka ku-56% ngoNtulikazi kuya kuMandulo 2020 ngo-23 % ngoLwezi 2022-Masingana 2023. Ukuqhubeka kokuphazamiseka kungenxa yakho kokubili ukufuneka nokusaplaya izinkinga eziseceleni, okufaka amazinga aphansi okunakekelwa kwezempilo emphakathini okudingayo kanye nomkhawulo wokubakhona kwabasebenzi bezempilo nezinsizakusebenza ezinye zokunakekelwa kwezempilo njengokuvulwa kwemitholampilo noma ukubakhona kwesitoko semithi nemikhiqizo. "Izindaba ezamukelwayo ukuthi izinhlelozempilo emzaweni amaningi ziqala ukuvuselela imisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile ezigidini zabantu abawugejile ngesikhathi sobhubhane," kusho uDkt Rudi Eggers, Umqondisi weWHO weMisebenzi yeZempilo yoKuhlanganisiwe. "Kodwa sidinga ukuqinisekisa ukuthi wonke amazwe ayaqhubeka nokuvala izikhala ukuvuselela imisebenzi yezempilo,nokusebenzisa izifundo abazifundile ukwakha izinhlelo zempilo ezizilungiselele nokubekezela kwekusasa". Izimpawu zokuqala eziqaphelekayo ekuvuseleleni Kulolu cwaningo olusha,amazwe ambalwa abike ngokungenhloso isikalo sasemuva sokuthola kuzinkundla zonke umsebenzi wokuletha nokusebenza kwezempilo yomphakathi okubalulekile kusukela 2020-2021 ukubikwa kukhombisa ukubaluleka kwegxathu kubuyelwe emazingeni angaphambi kobhubhane ukulethwa kokusebenza nokusebenza okukhulu kwezinhlelo. Ekupheleni kuka-2022, amazwe amaningi abike izimpawu ezingacacile zokuvuselelwa kokusebenza okufaka ukusebenza kwezocansi, ukuzala, abakhullwe, abazelwe, izingane nezempilo yentsha, ukudla okunempilo ukugomma,izifo ezithelelanayo (okufaka umalaleveva, iHIV, iTB nezinye izifo ezithelelana ngokocansi) izifo ezidumile ezinganakiwe,izifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo,ukuphathwa kwengqondo, okwengqondo nesifo sokusebenzisa izidakamiza, ukunakekelwa kwabantu abadala nokunakekelwa okulekelelayo nokwesiko. Inombolo yamazwe abika ukuphazamiseka ohlelweni lwawo lesaplayi sheni ezweni okungafika kuhhafu (29 ka-59 wamazwe aphendulayo) okunganekota (18 wa-66 wamazwe) aphedulayo kulonyaka odlule. Ngaphandle kwezinkomba zokuvuselela,ukuphazamiseka komsebenzi kuyaqhubeka emazweni onke namazinga emali engenayo kuzona zonke izinhlelo soomsebenzi wokulatha nokulandelela umsebenzi ezindaweni. Amazwe aphinde abhekane nokunyuka komsebenzi okumele wenziwe - ikakhulukazi emisebenzini yokubheka, ukuxilonga nokwelapha izifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo - okungaholela kumiphumela engemihle uma abantu bebambezeleka ukuthola ukunakekelwa ngesikhathi. Ukuvuselelwa kwemisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile kubucayi ngenxa yokuphazamiseka - okufaka imisebenzi yokugqugquzela ezempilo, ukuvinjwa kwezifo, ukwelapha, ukuvuselelwa kwesimilo nephaliyeshini - kungaba nokukhulu umthelela ohlukene wezempil nezinga lomuntu ukunobhubhane qobo ikakhulukazi kubantu babuthaka. Ukuhlanganisa imisebenzi yeCOVID-19 emisebenzini yezempilo ebalulekile elinye igxathu elibalulekile eliya ekuvuselelweni kohlelo noguquko,amazwe amaningi asebe nenqubekela phambili ekuhlanganiseni imisebenzi yeCOVID-19 endleleni yokuletha imisebenzi yezempilo. Cishe u-80%-90% wamazwe ahlanganyele ngokuphelele ekugonyweni kweCOVID-19 ukuxilonga nemisebenzi yokuphathwa kwecala kanye nemisebenzi yesimo sangemuva kweCOVID-19 endleleni yomsebenzi wokuletha. Kuyafana amazwe amaningi (u-80% wamazwe awu-83 aphendulile) abike okungenani okuncane ekukhuphukeni kokuthola amathuluzi abalulekile eCovid-19 (isibonelo ukuxilongwa kweCOVID-19, ukwelaphangxube, imigomo namathuluzi okuzivikela siqu - PPE), ngezinkinga zokusebenza kwezempilo nokwentuleka koxhasomali okumele izivimbo ezijwayeleke kakhulu. Ukuqhubeka kokusekwa kuyadingeka ukuvuselela,ukubekezela nokuzilungiselela Amazwe amaningi aseqalile asebenzisa asebekufundile ngesikhathi sobhubhane lweCOVID-19 okufaka ngezikhungo zenamba yokusbenza kokusha ukuphazamisa amasu endleleni yokulethwa kwezempilo. Lokhu kufaka ukubekwa kwezindlela zokwelapha ngefoni,ukugqugquzela ukunakekelwa usekhaya nokungenelela kokuzinakekela, izindlela zokuqinisa ukubakhona kwabasebenzi bezempilo, amandla nezindlela zokweseka, ukwenza kabusha ekuthengeni nokuletha imithi namasaplayi ezinye izindlela zokuxhumana nomphakathi ukubambisana nabanikezeli bomkhakha ozimele nowomphakathi. Amakota amathathu ezwe abaike ukufakwa koxhasomali olwengeziwe oluya ekuvuselelweni kohlelo lwesikhathi eside, ukubekezela nokuzilungiselela. Amazwe agcizelele isidingo sokwesekwa kweWHO ekubhekaneni nezinkinga ezisele ngokwengqikithi yeCOVID-19 nangale,ikakhulukazi okujwayele ukuhlobana nokuqinisa ukusebenza kwezempilo, ukwakha amandla okubheka imisebenzi yezempilo, ukwakha ukunakekelwa kwezempilo kokuqala okuncike kuzindlela zokunakekela, ukubusa, inqubomgomo nokuhlela nokuhlela ezezimali noxhasomali. Inothi kubahleli: Emzuliswaneni wesine wocwaningo lwephasile yomhlaba weWHO, amazwe angu-222, izindawo bamenywa ukuba baphendule kucwaningo oluncike kuwebhu eqinisikesiwe phakathi kukaLwezi 2022 noMasingana 2023. Ucwaningo lwesine lulandele kucwaningo lwephasile edlule yango-2020 no2021 lweWHO: uMzuliswano 1 (Nhlaba-Mandulo 2020) uMzuliswano 2 (Masingana-Ndasa 2021) noMzuliswano 3 (Lwezi-Zibandlela 2021) okwakhombisa ukukhulu bokuthi ubhubhane lalukhahlameza ukuqhubeka kwemisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile nokuthi amazwe enza kanjani.
